title: Freedom for All: what a liberal society could be [Book Review]
author: Berg-Sørensen

opening_move.description: The review opens by situating Zakaras's book in the context of intensifying attacks on liberal democracy and illiberalism, framing it as a timely defence of radical liberalism based on expansive equal freedom against critics from both left and right.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: A reconstructed radical liberalism based on expansive equal freedom can address the genuine failures of existing liberalism without abandoning liberal democracy

theoretical_positioning.description: Appreciative review presenting and contextualising Zakaras's radical liberal vision. The reviewer is broadly sympathetic, offering context and some critical observations about what the vision requires.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of Zakaras; contextualisation within liberalism debates
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review (4 pages). Primarily expository and appreciative. Reviewer has no strong independent normative position.

case_type.case_name: Zakaras, Freedom for All: What a Liberal Society Could Be (2025)

confidence_notes: Book review (4 pages). Primarily expository. Reviewer's framing in terms of contemporary illiberal challenge gives it more contextual richness than a pure summary.
